key_insight: Ethanol-assisted interfacial modification enables laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica without optical contact or mechanical clamping.
engineering_relevance: The approach simplifies joining of dissimilar composite–glass assemblies, potentially reducing fixture complexity and expanding weldability for precision manufacturing.
method: Treat the CFRP–fused silica interface with ethanol to modify optical/thermal coupling.
Apply laser transmission welding under non-contact, clamp-free conditions.
Use the modified interface to promote localized joining at the bond line.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article in Precision Engineering, but provides no quantitative performance data or evaluation metrics.
watch_item: Practical adoption may depend on controlling interface preparation and achieving consistent joint quality across different material surfaces.
paper_title: Non-optical-contact and clamp-free laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica via ethanol-assisted interfacial modification
